children.heading: Alaska Native
children.label: 1 Alaska Native
children.text: The term “Alaska Native” means a Native as such term is defined in .section 1602 of title 43

children.heading: Bureau-funded school
children.label: 2 Bureau-funded school
children.text: The term “Bureau-funded school” has the meaning given the term in .section 2021 of title 25

children.heading: Native Hawaiian
children.label: 3 Native Hawaiian
children.text: The term “Native Hawaiian” means any individual any of whose ancestors were natives, prior to 1778, of the area which now comprises the State of Hawaii.

children.heading: Native Hawaiian organization
children.label: 4 Native Hawaiian organization
children.text: The term “Native Hawaiian organization” has the meaning given the term in .section 7517 of this title

children.heading: Authority
children.label: 1 Authority
children.text: From funds reserved under , the Secretary shall make grants to or enter into contracts with Indian Tribes, Tribal organizations, and Alaska Native entities to carry out the authorized programs described in subsection (c), except that such grants or contracts shall not be awarded to secondary school programs in Bureau-funded schools.section 2321(a)(1)(B)(i) of this title

children.heading: Indian Tribes and Tribal organizations
children.label: 2 Indian Tribes and Tribal organizations
children.text: The grants or contracts described in this section that are awarded to any Indian Tribe or Tribal organization shall be subject to the terms and conditions of and shall be conducted in accordance with the provisions of sections 5345, 5346, and 5347 of title 25, which are relevant to the programs administered under this subsection.section 5321 of title 25

children.heading: Special authority relating to secondary schools operated or supported by the Bureau of Indian Education
children.label: 3 Special authority relating to secondary schools operated or supported by the Bureau of Indian Education
children.text: An Indian Tribe, a Tribal organization, or an Alaska Native entity, that receives funds through a grant made or contract entered into under paragraph (1) may use the funds to provide assistance to a secondary school operated or supported by the Bureau of Indian Education to enable such school to carry out career and technical education programs.

children.heading: Matching
children.label: 4 Matching
children.text: If sufficient funding is available, the Bureau of Indian Education shall expend an amount equal to the amount made available under this subsection, relating to programs for Indians, to pay a part of the costs of programs funded under this subsection. During each fiscal year the Bureau of Indian Education shall expend not less than the amount expended during the prior fiscal year on career and technical education programs, services, and technical activities administered directly by, or under contract with, the Bureau of Indian Education, except that in no year shall funding for such programs, services, and activities be provided from accounts and programs that support other Indian education programs. The Secretary and the Director of the Bureau of Indian Education shall prepare jointly a plan for the expenditure of funds made available and for the evaluation of programs assisted under this subsection. Upon the completion of a joint plan for the expenditure of the funds and the evaluation of the programs, the Secretary shall assume responsibility for the administration of the program, with the assistance and consultation of the Bureau of Indian Education.

children.label: A
children.text: confer with, and allow for active participation by, representatives of Indian Tribes, Tribal organizations, and individual Tribal members; and

children.label: B
children.text: promulgate the regulations under subchapter III of chapter 5 of title 5, commonly known as the “Negotiated Rulemaking Act of 1990”.

children.heading: Application
children.label: 6 Application
children.text: Any Indian Tribe, Tribal organization, or Bureau-funded school eligible to receive assistance under this subsection may apply individually or as part of a consortium with another such Indian Tribe, Tribal organization, or Bureau-funded school.

children.heading: Authorized programs
children.label: 1 Authorized programs
children.text: Funds made available under this section shall be used to carry out career and technical education programs consistent with the purpose of this chapter.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: 2 Special rule
children.text: Notwithstanding , funds made available under this section may be used to provide preparatory, refresher, and remedial education services that are designed to enable students to achieve success in career and technical education programs or programs of study.section 2302(5)(A)(iii) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Funds received pursuant to grants or contracts awarded under subsection (b) may be used to provide stipends to students who are enrolled in career and technical education programs and who have acute economic needs which cannot be met through work-study programs.

children.heading: Amount
children.label: B Amount
children.text: Stipends described in subparagraph (A) shall not exceed reasonable amounts as prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Grant or contract application
children.label: d Grant or contract application
children.text: In order to receive a grant or contract under this section, an organization, Tribe, or entity described in subsection (b) shall submit an application to the Secretary that shall include an assurance that such organization, Tribe, or entity shall comply with the requirements of this section.
programs that involve, coordinate with, or encourage Tribal economic development plans; and

children.label: A
children.text: are accredited or are candidates for accreditation by a nationally recognized accreditation organization as an institution of postsecondary career and technical education; or

children.label: B
children.text: operate career and technical education programs that are accredited or are candidates for accreditation by a nationally recognized accreditation organization, and issue certificates for completion of career and technical education programs.

children.heading: Consolidation of funds
children.label: f Consolidation of funds
children.text: Each organization, Tribe, or entity receiving assistance under this section may consolidate such assistance with assistance received from related programs in accordance with the provisions of the Indian Employment, Training and Related Services Demonstration Act of 1992 ( et seq.).25 U.S.C. 3401
to limit the eligibility of any organization, Tribe, or entity described in subsection (b) to participate in any activity offered by an eligible agency or eligible recipient under this subchapter; or

to preclude or discourage any agreement, between any organization, Tribe, or entity described in subsection (b) and any eligible agency or eligible recipient, to facilitate the provision of services by such eligible agency or eligible recipient to the population served by such eligible agency or eligible recipient.

children.heading: Native Hawaiian programs
children.label: h Native Hawaiian programs
children.text: From the funds reserved pursuant to , the Secretary shall award grants to or enter into contracts with community-based organizations primarily serving and representing Native Hawaiians to plan, conduct, and administer programs, or portions thereof, which are authorized by and consistent with the provisions of this section for the benefit of Native Hawaiians.section 2321(a)(1)(B)(ii) of this title
